deep_dives.title: Physics Models → Market Alpha: When Scientists Become Traders
deep_dives.thesis: 26 posts map physics/math models to trading — avalanche theory, Ising models, phase space reconstruction. The meta-insight: the quant edge isn't in finance, it's in importing models from other fields.

deep_dives.action_sketch: Study the cross-disciplinary models. Build a signal ensemble that combines 3+ physics-derived indicators. Backtest with Monte Carlo validation. Deploy as an AI agent that runs daily.

deep_dives.title: Why Your Agent Sucks: Architecture Patterns That Actually Work

deep_dives.title: 25 Agents Walk Into a Market: AI-Powered Trading Infrastructure

deep_dives.title: The Claude Harness Pattern: Context, Memory, and Cognitive Scaffolding
deep_dives.thesis: The most effective Claude users aren't writing better prompts — they're building harnesses: layered systems of CLAUDE.md files, skills, hooks, and memory that turn a chat window into a persistent, self-improving agent. This pattern emerged independently across multiple creators and maps directly to how production AI systems are being architected.
deep_dives.action_sketch:
1. Audit your current Claude setup — do you have a CLAUDE.md? Skills? Hooks?
2. Map your workflow: what context does Claude need every session vs. occasionally?
3. Build a minimal harness: CLAUDE.md (permanent rules) + primer.md (session state) + skills/ (reusable workflows)
4. Add memory persistence: file-based or MCP-backed cross-session recall
5. Layer in hooks for self-correction (caveman mode, token discipline, audit-after-step)

deep_dives.title: MediaPipe × TouchDesigner: Real-Time Body-to-Art Pipeline
deep_dives.thesis: MediaPipe's body/hand/face tracking combined with TouchDesigner's node-based visual programming creates a real-time pipeline where human movement becomes generative art. Multiple creators are independently converging on this stack — from spider-web string simulations to 3D Gaussian splatting to interactive music visualizers.
deep_dives.action_sketch:
1. Install TouchDesigner + MediaPipe Python bindings
2. Set up basic hand landmark tracking (21 points per hand)
3. Pipe landmark data into TouchDesigner via OSC or shared memory
4. Build a simple particle system that responds to hand position
5. Layer in face mesh (468 landmarks) for expression-reactive visuals
6. Export as standalone for installations or stream via NDI

deep_dives.title: Beyond Prompting: Prompt Architecture as Engineering Discipline
deep_dives.thesis: Prompt engineering is dead. What's replacing it is prompt architecture — the systematic design of context windows, memory layers, retrieval systems, and cognitive scaffolds. The shift from 'write a better prompt' to 'build a better system around the model' is the biggest unlock in practical AI right now.

deep_dives.action_sketch:
1. Stop optimizing prompts in isolation — map the full context flow
2. Implement RAG with hybrid search + reranking for internal docs
3. Build a memory layer: what the model needs to remember across sessions
4. Add retrieval-augmented generation for domain-specific knowledge
5. Design self-correction: let the model audit its own output
6. Measure: track which context components actually improve output quality

deep_dives.title: Autonomous Agents in the Wild: What's Actually Working in 2026
deep_dives.thesis: Autonomous agents went from demo-ware to daily drivers in 2026. But the gap between 'impressive demo' and 'reliable tool' is massive. These entries map the frontier: Qwen agents roaming NYC streets, Claude automating daily workflows, Kronos failing hedge fund stress tests. The pattern: narrow agents with clear guardrails work; general agents with vague goals don't.
deep_dives.action_sketch:
1. Start narrow: one task, one tool, one feedback loop
2. Build in guardrails before capability (kill switches, budget limits, scope constraints)
3. Test adversarially: what happens when the agent encounters edge cases?
4. Add persistence: agents need memory to avoid repeating mistakes
5. Monitor: log everything, review regularly, prune bad patterns
6. Scale horizontally: multiple narrow agents > one general agent

deep_dives.title: Claude Mastery: From First Install to Advanced Agent Architecture
deep_dives.thesis: Claude Code is not a chatbot — it's a programmable agent framework. The gap between 'using Claude' and 'mastering Claude' is a stack of learnable patterns: CLAUDE.md configuration, skills/hooks/MCP extensibility, memory persistence, cognitive scaffolding, prompt architecture, parallel worktrees, and multi-agent orchestration. This guide maps every layer with dated, sourced entries that track the evolution from Opus 4.6 through Fable 5.
deep_dives.action_sketch:
1. Install Claude Code: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
2. Create CLAUDE.md in your project root with behavioral rules
3. Install 2-3 starter skills (brainstorming, systematic-debugging)
4. Set up auto-memory (file-based or MCP-backed)
5. Add hooks for session-start context loading
6. Learn thinking keywords (think → ultrathink progression)
7. Try parallel worktrees for multi-branch development
8. Build custom skills for your recurring workflows

deep_dives.title: Prompt Engineering Isn't Dead — It Just Grew Up

deep_dives.title: The Incentive Trap: Game Theory for the AI Age
deep_dives.thesis: Game theory isn't abstract math — it's the operating system behind every AI product decision, every market move, and every platform play. The entries in this collection reveal the incentive structures hiding in plain sight.

connection_map.DXprHQ8Dg3G: The incentive trap: how $1 commitments create billion-dollar lock-in
connection_map.DXiR_RyCF8s: Failed strategies: what happens when you ignore the game-theoretic structure
connection_map.DV6c9ItjPZp: AI's origin game: decades of academic incentives that produced the current landscape
connection_map.DRzrIiDDjN5: Physics as game theory: everything reduces to forces and equilibria
connection_map.DXMt7CtEeEo: Optimization puzzles: the mathematical backbone of strategic thinking
connection_map.DXcs7NJiSy1: Radiation patterns: physical systems as strategic actors

deep_dives.action_sketch: Map the incentive structure before making any decision → identify who benefits from each outcome → look for lock-in mechanisms (Elon's $1 pattern) → study failed strategies to understand what the 'game' actually is → apply physics intuition (equilibria, forces) to strategic situations.

deep_dives.title: Art Without Gatekeepers: How AI is Rebuilding the Creative Economy
deep_dives.thesis: The art market's gatekeeping structure — galleries, curators, collectors, degree requirements — is being dismantled by AI tools and direct distribution. Artists who adapt are finding new revenue streams (AI-assisted commissions, generative art NFTs, interactive installations). Those who resist are watching their distribution channels commoditize around them.

deep_dives.title: Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow — parallel worktrees + plan mode
deep_dives.summary: @evolving.ai summarizes Boris Cherny's X thread on how the Anthropic team actually uses Claude Code: 3-5 git worktrees in parallel, start every complex task in plan mode, update rules after every mistake, build reusable skills and subagents.

deep_dives.title: Boris Cherny's personal CLAUDE.md — plan-first, self-improving rules
deep_dives.summary: @keshavsuki dissects Boris Cherny's (Anthropic) personal CLAUDE.md: plan-first, subagents for complex work, self-improving lessons.md loop, hard verification standard, elegance demand, autonomous bug fixing, git discipline. High-signal template for CLAUDE.md design.

deep_dives.title: primer.md + memory.sh — live-updating CLAUDE.md companion
deep_dives.summary: @keshavsuki on fixing CLAUDE.md staleness: add a primer.md that Claude rewrites at end of every session (current state, next step, blockers), plus a memory.sh launch hook that injects last 5 commits and modified files, plus a git hook logging commits. Four-layer always-current context.

deep_dives.title: 7 must-install Claude Code repos — Obsidian Skills, n8n-MCP, Superpowers

deep_dives.title: 10 non-cliche quant research projects — Bayesian BL, RL factors, PBO
deep_dives.summary: @heidioftheeast's 10 quant project ideas beyond Black-Scholes: probability of backtest overfitting, Bayesian Black-Litterman, survival-based risk, RL factor portfolios, Sharpe statistics, convex portfolio trading, TS vs XS factors, numeraire-invariant hedging, exotic option model risk, regime-switching factors.

deep_dives.title: 10 institutional quant projects — Avellaneda-Stoikov, Hawkes, HMM
deep_dives.summary: @heidioftheeast's deeper 10-project list: Avellaneda-Stoikov market maker, multivariate Hawkes order-flow, Almgren-Chriss cross-impact, eigenportfolios via random matrix theory, realized-kernel vol forecasting, meta-labeling, orthogonal alpha stacking, HMM regime detection, adversarial stress tests, capacity/impact scaling. Serious portfolio builder.

deep_dives.title: Anthropic Claude Code source-code leak — scale commentary
deep_dives.summary: @gojutechtalk framing the Anthropic Claude Code source leak as potentially the largest source-code leak ever. Reference for 'the leak' as a discussion point.

deep_dives.title: Claude Code hooks — 3 daily-driver hooks from @jens.heitmann
deep_dives.summary: @jens.heitmann on Claude Code hooks being under-utilized, with his 3 daily hooks. Pointer to Jens's CC hooks content; specifics live in the video.

deep_dives.title: Polymarket 99c limit-order edge — 75 automated trades/hour
deep_dives.summary: @beinginvested on a Polymarket trader placing limit orders on outcomes already closing at 99c, automated at 75 trades/hour, 4 months to crack it. Teaser with DM-gated full breakdown; worth remembering the pattern.

deep_dives.title: OpenScreen — free open-source Screen Studio alternative
deep_dives.summary: @marc.kaz on OpenScreen (github.com/siddharthvaddem/openscreen): free MIT-licensed screen recorder with auto-zoom on clicks, cursor animations, webcam overlays, any aspect ratio. Plus the Recordly fork with native Mac/Windows recording.

deep_dives.title: Hunyuan3D-2 — Tencent's free image-to-3D model generator
deep_dives.summary: Tencent's Hunyuan3D-2 turns text or an uploaded image into textured 3D models in seconds. Free web UI; pipeline is upload > Generate Shape > Generate Textured Shape > download. Good for game/app asset workflows.

deep_dives.title: World Labs — explorable 3D worlds from a single 2D image
deep_dives.summary: @rpn covers World Labs' first demo: generate navigable 3D worlds from one image or sentence, with camera controls and style effects. Referenced as a likely Quest/Vision Pro use case.
